entries.source: 1. Go to api.slack.com/apps > Create New App > From Scratch 2. Add Bot Token Scopes (channels:read, chat:write, etc.) 3. Install to workspace, copy the Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...) 4. Team ID is in your workspace URL or Slack admin settings
1. Go to brave.com/search/api 2. Sign up for a free plan (2000 queries/month) 3. Copy your API key

1. Go to github.com > Settings > Developer Settings > Personal Access Tokens > Fine-grained tokens 2. Generate a new token with the repo permissions you need 3. Paste it as Github Personal Access Token
1. Go to linear.app > Settings > API 2. Create a Personal API Key 3. Paste it as Linear Api Key

1. Go to notion.so/my-integrations 2. Create a new integration 3. Copy the Internal Integration Secret 4. Share the Notion pages/databases you want accessible with the integration 5. For Openapi Mcp Headers enter: {"Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_SECRET", "Notion-Version": "2022-06-28"}
1. Go to todoist.com > Settings > Integrations > Developer 2. Copy your API token
A C/C++ compiler (build-essential / base-devel) is required to compile llama.cpp.
A dependency failed to build during install — usually an older package whose build breaks on this Python version, not a server problem. The install did not finish.
A dependent service (embedding, vector DB, calendar sync) is unavailable.
A fresh Agent session for this window only.
a fresh blank document — click it to try it out! (Or hit
A hands-on course teaching you to understand, operate, troubleshoot, and safely extend OpenClank and Copal. Every lesson maps to the actual current app.

All active chat sessions. Click to open.

All eight categories above, in one go. Same effect as wiping each one in sequence.
All external service connections in one place.

(all hex colors). Optional advanced color key=value pairs (userBubbleBg, aiBubbleBg, bubbleBorder, sidebarBg, sectionAccent, brandColor, inputBg, inputBorder, sendBtnBg, sendBtnHover, codeBg, codeFg, toggleBg, toggleActive, accentPrimary, accentError). Optional background EFFECTS: bgPattern=

and can reach outside it. A workspace scopes the tools; it is not a security boundary.
and shown across Galaxy, Timeline & Calendar. Add more than one track to make it a shared (🔗) event.
Another process is using the configured port.
Another window saved this note first. Nothing was overwritten; compare both versions and choose explicitly.

API key (optional — for protected local endpoints)

Apply an art style to the image using img2img. Requires a running diffusion model.
Archived sessions. Restore to make active again.
Ask an admin to configure model endpoints
Audit all publishes passing, necessary skills at or above this confidence. Off = keep audit results as drafts unless manually approved.
Audit {0}? Each is tested from top to bottom, then published or moved to draft using your auto-approve confidence threshold.
Author, publish, enroll, navigate, and complete durable learning paths.
Authorization timed out. Reconnect from the server list to retry.
Authorize in the opened tab. If the redirect fails (remote access), paste the resulting URL here:
Auto lets the LLM pick the output shape. Override when you specifically want a Compare table, How-to, Product, or Fact-check.
Auto-register endpoint error: {0}. Use ⋮ → Register endpoint to retry.
Auto-register endpoint failed ({0}). Use ⋮ → Register endpoint to retry.
Auto tool choice needs a tool call parser.
Automatically draft reusable skills from your workflows. Audit all can publish passing skills using the threshold below.
AWQ/GPTQ/FP8 needs a working vLLM/SGLang accelerator path; this server did not expose one.
Background Remove needs the package on this server. Click to install it from Cookbook → Dependencies.
Bases, Tasks & Calendar — Structured data, task management, CalDAV sync
Best duplicate candidate by published status, uses, confidence, and specificity

Blends pasted layers into the base photo. Color match shifts the layer's lighting/tone to match its surroundings (no pixel redraw). Seam fix uses inpaint to clean jagged cutout edges (needs a self-hosted img2img/inpaint model).
Blind Mode — hide model names until you vote
Blur emails, tokens, and secrets in AI output
Blurs the inpaint result's alpha edge — drag to blend the AI fill into the surrounding image. Updates live.
Bookmark a note from its actions menu.
Brain tour: memories, tidy, skills, settings
Break this event into ordered steps (e.g. Book → Prep → Visit). They show as a checklist in the side panel and a progress bar on the timeline chip.
Break this event into ordered steps (e.g. Book → Prep → Visit). They show as a checklist here and a progress bar on the timeline chip.

Browser automation via Playwright. The AI can navigate pages, click, fill forms, and read content. Runs headless by default. Remove --headless from Args to see the browser window. First run installs Chromium automatically.
Browsers can’t read folders dropped from native file managers (Thunar/Nautilus). Use the "Upload album" tile in the Albums tab instead.
Brush diameter (log scale 1→800px). Use [ and ] for ±10%.

Brush the area you want the AI to redraw — the red preview marks the mask region. Use Paint to add, Erase to subtract (or hold Ctrl+Alt to flip for one stroke). Generate fills with what your prompt describes; Remove fills with the surrounding background.
Build Copal Knowledge — Documents, Bases, Timeline verification
Built-in Tools — File, shell, knowledge, orchestration tools
by pasting model link, or download directly in the Scan section below.
Calculate and use suggested context from scanned hardware
Can't launch: GPU driver error — {0}. Reinstall or repair the NVIDIA driver, then re-scan.
Can't launch: no GPU detected by nvidia-smi. {0} needs a working CUDA or ROCm device.
Can't launch: {0} isn't installed{1}. Install it first: {2}
Can't launch: {0} needs {1} ≥ {2}, but {3} is installed{4}. Upgrade: {5}
Cancel an in-progress model download by killing its tmux session. Use list_downloads first to get the session_id.
Capture, review, promote, and recall memory. Understand trust boundaries.

Check for valid JSON. If corrupted, restore from backup or create a fresh state.

Check or change the port in or via env var.
Check provider status. Try a different model. Check the Diagnostics view for errors.
Check terminal output for startup errors. Verify the URL matches the configured port.
— check the one downloads should go to

Clears `memory.json`, the Memory table, and the vector store. Skills not affected.
Click a region to select similar pixels. Shift+click to add, Alt+click to subtract. Esc to clear.
Click a shortcut to rebind. Press Escape to cancel.
Click a tag to filter. Tasks with no tags are always visible.

Context length. Lower it to find more models that could fit your hardware; raise it when you need longer chats or documents.

Controls for the agent tool loop.
Controls how fired note reminders are delivered.
Controls how many relevant published or approved skills are added to each agent request.
Controls randomness. Lower values give focused, deterministic answers (good for code). Higher values give more creative, varied responses.
Copal validates the archive before committing imported records.

Create a local calendar, import an .ics file, or sync via CalDAV.
Create a new chat for ongoing conversations with a specific model. (The UI calls these 'chats'; 'session' is the internal term.)

Create a new document in the editor panel. Use this when the user asks to write, create, build, make, or generate code, scripts, programs, games, apps, or any long-form or structured content that is more than a short paragraph, AND there is no already-open document/email draft that the request refers to. If an email compose draft is open, edit that draft instead of creating another document. NEVER put large generated content directly in chat — use this tool instead.
Create email reply drafts without sending
Create, update, delete, and toggle todo items

Create, update, delete, or list the user's CardDAV contacts. Use to save a new contact, update an existing one (email/phone/address), or remove one. Add does not require email: name + phone or name + address is valid. For update/delete you need the contact's uid — call action='list' first to find it. Writes go through the same dedupe + validation as the Contacts UI.

Edit a document OPEN IN THE EDITOR PANEL (created via create_document) — NOT a file on disk. For files on disk (home folder, project files, anything with a path like ~/x.txt or /path/to/file) use edit_file instead. Targeted find-and-replace with multiple FIND/REPLACE pairs per call; use for any edit smaller than a full rewrite. Do NOT send the whole file back via update_document for small edits.

Edit a file ON DISK by exact string replacement (home folder, project files, any real path like ~/sweden.txt or /path/to/file). This is the right tool for files on disk — NOT edit_document (that's for editor-panel documents). PREFER this over bash (sed/echo) — it shows a diff. old_string must match the file exactly and be unique (or set replace_all). Use write_file to create a new file.
Edit a gallery image: upscale, remove background, inpaint, or harmonize.
Edit/delete the task in the Tasks tab — this event will follow.
Edit needs line 2: memory_id, line 3: new text
Edit text and save, new chats will have the new context. Regenerate or continue from there.
Edit the Args field to change which directory the server has access to.
Edit the frontmatter and body directly. Save replaces the file via PUT /api/skills/{name}.
Edit this event in the popup.
Enable Nobody mode — no memory, no history saved
Enable or disable tools available to the AI agent.
Enable Temporary Agent — no memory or history saved

FlashInfer is JIT-compiling sampling kernels with an nvcc too old for this GPU (no sm_89 / sm_90 support — pre-CUDA 11.8). Changing the attention backend does not help — flashinfer JITs the SAMPLER too. The clean fix is to set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=0 so vLLM uses its native sampler instead.

Hold Ctrl+Alt to flip temporarily for a single stroke.
How long the researcher waits for a single URL to fetch and extract before giving up on it. Slow sites get skipped. Default 90 seconds.
How many search → read → reflect rounds the agent runs. More rounds = deeper coverage, longer wait, more tokens.
How many URLs the researcher fetches and extracts in parallel. Higher is faster but uses more memory/CPU. Default 3.
How many web search results to fetch per query
How much of the Reinhard color/luminance shift to apply. 0 = no shift, 1 = fully match surroundings.

How much the AI redraws inside the mask. 0 = no change · 1 = full re-generation from your prompt. Recommended: 0.9–1.0 to add/replace an object, 0.6–0.8 to change material or color, 0.3–0.5 for subtle touch-ups. Default 0.75 works for most edits.
How much the AI redraws inside the mask (0 = no change, 1 = full diffusion).
How the next click combines with the current selection. Shift / Alt held during a click override this for one click.

Live-applied to the last Bg Removed layer. Feather softens the edge; Edge nudges it inward (−) or outward (+).
Live diagnostic logs and system output from the Open Clank process.

Live edge trimming for the last Inpaint Result layer. Edge feather softens the alpha boundary; Edge stroke expands (+) or contracts (−) the visible edge into the AI buffer that was generated around your brush.
Live from the memory engine — this is exactly what the AI receives about your memories at the start of every chat turn, split the same way it's injected. Nothing here is hardcoded.

Lower quant tiers (Q2/Q3/Q4 / AWQ-4bit) are smaller, faster, and cheaper to run, at some quality loss. Higher tiers (Q8 / FP8 / FP16 / BF16) preserve more quality but need more VRAM. “All” shows the best-scoring quant per model — pick a specific one to filter.

MTP / speculative decoding is supported on a few model families only — turn it on when the model card explicitly recommends it. On supported models it can boost inference throughput up to ~3×; on unsupported models it will either be ignored or fail to launch.

Rule of thumb: GGUF on single GPU / CPU+RAM → llama.cpp (or Ollama). Safetensors on multi-GPU NVIDIA → vLLM. SGLang is a vLLM-class alternative, sometimes faster on big-MoE / long-context.

Search API used for web search and deep research.

Search file contents for a regular expression across a directory tree (uses ripgrep when available, respecting .gitignore). Returns file:line:match. PREFER this over `bash grep/rg` for code search — confined to the allowed roots, structured output.
Search HuggingFace for models matching a query. Returns a ranked list of repo IDs, sizes (when available), and download counts. Use this when the user wants to find a model to download.
Search keyword(s) to find in past conversations

Search the user's past session transcripts by keyword. Use when the user asks about previous chats, past conversations, or when direct transcript evidence is better than persistent memory. Returns matching sessions with clickable links and nearby context.
Search the web for the current population of the 3 largest cities in the world, then calculate what percentage of the world's total population lives in those cities.
Searches the web for relevant information to include in the response. Results are fetched and summarized before the AI answers.

Setup: 1. Go to console.cloud.google.com > create or select a project 2. APIs & Services > Library > search "Gmail API" > Enable 3. APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen > set up (External is fine) 4. Under Audience, add your Gmail address as a test user 5. APIs & Services > Credentials > + Create Credentials > OAuth Client ID 6. Application type: Desktop App > Create 7. Copy the Client ID and Client Secret into the fields above 8. Click Add Server, then click the Authorize button 9. Sign in with Google, copy the URL from the error page, paste it back

Setup: 1. Go to console.cloud.google.com > create/select a project 2. APIs & Services > Library > enable Google Calendar API 3. APIs & Services > Credentials > + Create Credentials > OAuth Client ID 4. Application type: Desktop App > Create 5. Click "Download JSON" on the credential you just created 6. Set Google Oauth Credentials to the full path of the downloaded JSON file

Suggested action: check the captured output for the package that failed to build; it may need a newer release or a patch to install on this Python version.
Suggested action: disable SGLang decode CUDA graph for this launch. DeepSeek-V4 is reaching graph capture, but this kernel is failing on the target hardware.

Suggested action: install cmake via the OS package manager — apt: cmake build-essential / pacman: cmake base-devel / dnf: cmake gcc-c++ make / brew: cmake. Cookbook can do this automatically on the next launch if your user has passwordless sudo for apt/pacman/dnf.
Suggested action: read the captured output for the failing step; copy the troubleshooting bundle if you need help.
Suggested action: relaunch with Odysseus’ venv CUDA library path fix. If the venv does not contain the matching NVIDIA runtime libs, run Repair sglang-kernel.
Suggested action: relaunch with the updated runner so llama.cpp builds CPU-only, or install a complete CUDA toolkit/runtime on this server for GPU llama.cpp.
Suggested action: relaunch with VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=0 prepended. (Confirmed on the QuantTrio/Qwen3.5 model card as the canonical workaround.)
Suggested action: retry with a lower tensor-parallel size, such as TP=4 or TP=2. If it still fails, use a non-FP8/GGUF version of the model.
Suggested action: run Clear GPUs, then relaunch. If it still fails, choose only equally free GPUs or lower TP/context.

Suggested action: stop retrying graph/memory tweaks for this exact FP8 command. SGLang’s RTX PRO 6000 recipe uses the original deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash checkpoint with --moe-runner-backend marlin, not the converted sgl-project FP8 checkpoint. Try that recipe/checkpoint, official SGLang container/nightly, or supported Hopper/Blackwell hardware.
Suggested action: switch this serve config to llama.cpp for CPU/local serving, or choose a GPU server.
Suggested action: switch this serve config to Ollama (highly recommended, has dynamic CPU fallbacks), or choose a remote Linux GPU server.
Suggested action: upgrade vLLM through the environment that provides this CLI (package manager, venv, Docker image, or source checkout), or choose a compatible checkpoint.

The default persona is who the assistant is everywhere — chat, the personal assistant, reminders, and background work; edits to it sync to all of them. Saved and built-in characters can be managed here too; enable one for a chat from the chat bar's persona modal.
The elevator-pitch index of everything else — headlines only, wrapped as untrusted data. The AI pulls full text with its recall tool when a topic matters.
The HTTP header name the key goes under (Miniflux: X-Auth-Token; most others: Authorization). Only used when Auth = Header.
The library can grow; cleanup retires weak/duplicate skills only after review.

Your name as it appears in the From: field of emails you send, e.g. Jane Smith. Auto-filled from Google during OAuth.
Your outgoing-mail server, e.g. smtp.gmail.com. Leave blank to make this account read-only.
Your outgoing-mail server, e.g. smtp.gmail.com, smtp.migadu.com. Leave blank to make this account read-only.
Your SMTP password — often the same as your IMAP password. Outlook / Office 365 generally requires OAuth and will not work with a normal password here.
